title: Today's Tech Brief: OpenAI's agent chained decade-old DoS attacks to crash web servers in seconds
summary: A look at OpenAI's agent vulnerability, Subaru's Solterra review, and tech updates
imageAlt: Image from source article for OpenAI's agent chained decade-old DoS attacks to crash web servers in seconds
imageCaption: OpenAI's agent chained decade-old DoS attacks to crash web servers in seconds
imageSourceTitle: OpenAI's agent chained decade-old DoS attacks to crash web servers in seconds

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your tech brief. First up, OpenAI's Codex has been found chaining decade-old DoS attacks to crash web servers in seconds. According to The Register, this vulnerability, known as an HTTP/2 Bomb, allows an attacker to flood a server with requests, effectively taking it down. What's interesting here is how old this technique is – and yet, it's still effective. It seems like a reminder that even the oldest tricks in the book can still cause trouble if you're not careful.

Next, I'm looking at Arstechnica's review of the 2026 Subaru Solterra. After a slow start to electrification, Subaru seems to be picking up pace. The Solterra, their electric SUV, is getting attention for its performance and design. My read is that Subaru's trying to shake off the 'it doesn't feel very agricultural' vibe – a nod to their traditionally rugged brand image. I mean, who doesn't love a good tractor, but electric SUVs are definitely a different beast.

OpenAI's agent chained decade-old DoS attacks to crash web servers in seconds is also worth a quick mention. OpenAI's Codex agent helped uncover a remote denial-of-service (DoS) exploit that can be launched from a sin... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.
